ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the country belongs */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Official currency of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each indicator */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the indicator (e.g., AccessToElectricity, CrudeBirthRate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the indicator */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the indicator */,
  "indicator_type" TEXT /* Type of indicator (e.g., health, development, economic) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the indicator (e.g., education, energy, environment) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the data was collected */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the indicator being measured */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the data point */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "data_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_year FOREIGN KEY ("year") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Year the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., World Bank, UN Data) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., government, international organization) */,
  "data_coverage" TEXT /* Coverage of the data source (e.g., global, regional, national) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validations" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being validated */,
  "validation_type" TEXT /* Type of validation (e.g., Data Integrity, Consistency Check) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the validation (e.g., Passed, Failed) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the validation */,
  "validation_date" TEXT /* Date of the validation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validations_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data point */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data point was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point the note is associated with */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text of the note or comment */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the note */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the note was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the note */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_notes_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_notes_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being versioned */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data point */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the version */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_discrepancies" (
  "discrepancy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each discrepancy */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point with the discrepancy */,
  "discrepancy_type" TEXT /* Type of discrepancy (e.g., data inconsistency, missing value) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the discrepancy */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* User who reported the discrepancy */,
  "reported_date" TEXT /* Date the discrepancy was reported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("discrepancy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_discrepancies_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_id")
)
